title: intensely earthy slovakian millet gratin
description: a keto-friendly, earthy, one-pot dish from slovakia, featuring millet that's been caramelized to perfection. the molten, cheesy top will pull you in and leave you wanting more.

ingredients.name: mushrooms
ingredients.notes: sliced, such as cremini or shiitake

instructions: in a large, oven-safe skillet, melt 1 tablespoon of the butter over medium heat.
add the sliced onion and cook, stirring occasionally, until caramelized and golden brown, about 20 minutes.
add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
add the sliced mushrooms and cook, stirring occasionally, until they release their liquid and start to brown, about 5 minutes.
add the flour and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
add the dry white wine and beef broth, stirring to deglaze the pan and scrape up any browned bits.
bring the mixture to a simmer and cook until it has reduced by half, about 5 minutes.
stir in the grated gruyere cheese until melted and smooth.
add the millet and stir to combine.
dot the top of the millet mixture with the remaining 1 tablespoon of butter.
transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and bake, uncovered, until the top is golden brown and the millet is tender, about 25-30 minutes.
remove the skillet from the oven and sprinkle the grated parmesan cheese on top.
return the skillet to the oven and bake for an additional 2-3 minutes, until the cheese is melted and bubbly.
remove the skillet from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes before serving.
serve hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
